evidence.reference_title: Practical Considerations for the Diagnosis and Management of Isovaleryl-CoA-Dehydrogenase Deficiency (Isovaleric Acidemia): Systematic Search and Review and Expert Opinions.
evidence.snippet:
Isovaleric acidemia (IVA, OMIM 243500) is an inherited disorder of leucine 
metabolism caused by a deficiency of isovaleryl-CoA dehydrogenase (IVD), leading 
to an accumulation of isovaleric acid and its derivates 3-hydroxyisovaleric 
acid, isovaleryl (C5)-carnitine and isovalerylglycine in body fluids.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated 3-hydroxyisovaleric acid reports alternate metabolism of the accumulated isovaleryl-CoA pool downstream of IVD deficiency.
readouts.target: Toxic isovaleryl-CoA and organic acid accumulation

nodes.description: Emergency management during catabolic crises includes cessation of protein intake, high-calorie glucose infusion to reverse catabolism, correction of metabolic acidosis, and administration of ammonia scavengers when needed.

evidence.reference_title: Practical Considerations for the Diagnosis and Management of Isovaleryl-CoA-Dehydrogenase Deficiency (Isovaleric Acidemia): Systematic Search and Review and Expert Opinions.
evidence.snippet:
Treatment consists of a protein-restricted diet 
combined with supplementation of carnitine and/or glycine and emergency 
treatment in catabolic episodes.

evidence.reference_title: Hyperammonaemia in classic organic acidaemias: a review of the literature and two case histories.
evidence.snippet:
The acute management of hyperammonaemia in organic 
acidaemias requires administration of intravenous calories as glucose and lipids 
to promote anabolism, carnitine to promote urinary excretion of urinary organic 
acid esters, and correction of metabolic acidosis

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric Acidemia in Jordan.
evidence.snippet:
Laboratory results showed acidosis (81%), 
hyperammonemia (71.4%), and hypoglycemia (14.3%).

evidence.reference_title: Hyperammonaemia in classic organic acidaemias: a review of the literature and two case histories.
evidence.snippet:
This is frequently accompanied by severe 
hyperammonaemia and constitutes a metabolic emergency

readouts.interpretation: Elevated ammonia reports secondary urea-cycle impairment during IVA metabolic decompensation.
readouts.target: Secondary urea cycle impairment and hyperammonemia

nodes.description: Fasting, febrile illness, gastroenteritis, and increased protein intake increase leucine flux and toxic organic-acid burden, triggering acute metabolic decompensation with vomiting, encephalopathy, metabolic acidosis, ketosis, hyperammonemia, and neurologic deterioration.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Isovaleric Acidemia.
evidence.snippet:
Classic IVA is characterized by acute metabolic
decompensations (vomiting, poor feeding, lethargy, hypotonia, seizures, and a
distinct odor of sweaty feet).

evidence.reference_title: Classic Isovaleric Acidemia.
evidence.snippet:
Acute metabolic decompensations are typically
triggered by fasting, (febrile) illness (especially gastroenteritis), or
increased protein intake.

nodes.description: A distinctive sweaty feet or cheesy odor due to volatile isovaleric acid accumulation is a classic clinical feature of IVA, particularly during acute metabolic crises.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric acidemia presenting as diabetic ketoacidosis: a case report.
evidence.snippet:
Isovaleric acidemia (IVA) is characterized by periodic vomiting, lethargy, coma,
ketoacidosis and a 'sweaty feet' odor.

nodes.description: IVD deficiency blocks leucine degradation at the mitochondrial isovaleryl-CoA to 3-methylcrotonyl-CoA step, causing upstream accumulation of isovaleryl-CoA, isovaleric acid, and characteristic secondary metabolites.

evidence.reference_title: Organic acid disorders.
evidence.snippet:
Organic acids (OAs) are intermediary products of several amino acid catabolism 
or degradation via multiple biochemical pathways for energy production.

nodes.description: Acute encephalopathy occurs during severe metabolic crises, mediated by hyperammonemia, direct organic acid neurotoxicity, and impaired neuronal energy metabolism. In a Jordanian cohort, encephalopathy occurred in 33.3%.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric Acidemia in Jordan.
evidence.snippet:
Vomiting was the most prevalent symptom (57.1%), and 
encephalopathy occurred in 33.3%.

evidence.reference_title: Creatine administration prevents Na+,K+-ATPase inhibition induced by intracerebroventricular administration of isovaleric acid in cerebral cortex of young rats.
evidence.snippet:
Patients affected by IVAcidemia suffer 
from acute episodes of encephalopathy, whose underlying mechanisms are poorly 
known.

nodes.description: Growth impairment can occur in patients with recurrent or poorly controlled disease due to chronic metabolic instability and dietary protein restriction.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric acidemia: Therapeutic response to supplementation with glycine, l-carnitine, or both in combination and a 10-year follow-up case study.
evidence.snippet: The chronic intermittent form with onset in infancy or childhood presents with developmental delays and/or failure to thrive.

nodes.description: Poor feeding can occur during acute metabolic decompensation in classic IVA, alongside vomiting, lethargy, hypotonia, seizures, and the characteristic odor.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Isovaleric Acidemia.
evidence.snippet:
Classic IVA is characterized by acute metabolic
decompensations (vomiting, poor feeding, lethargy, hypotonia, seizures, and a
distinct odor of sweaty feet).

evidence.reference_title: L-carnitine therapy in isovaleric acidemia.
evidence.snippet:
Isovaleric acidemia, resulting from isovaleryl-coenzyme A dehydrogenase
deficiency, is associated with marked reduction of free carnitine in both plasma
and urine.

readouts.interpretation: Low free carnitine reflects depletion of the free carnitine pool as accumulated isovaleryl-CoA is detoxified through acylcarnitine formation and urinary excretion.
readouts.target: Toxic isovaleryl-CoA and organic acid accumulation

nodes.description: Developmental delay is a risk in severe or recurrent disease, particularly following repeated hyperammonemic episodes.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Isovaleric Acidemia.
evidence.snippet:
Additional manifestations of classic IVA include developmental
delay, intellectual disability and/or impaired cognition, epilepsy, and movement
disorder (tremor, dysmetria, extrapyramidal movements).

nodes.description: Glycine supplementation promotes conjugation of isovaleryl-CoA to isovalerylglycine, a less toxic water-soluble metabolite excreted in urine. However, glycine supplementation should be carefully considered as increased glycine concentration does not proportionally increase N-isovalerylglycine formation.

evidence.reference_title: Practical Considerations for the Diagnosis and Management of Isovaleryl-CoA-Dehydrogenase Deficiency (Isovaleric Acidemia): Systematic Search and Review and Expert Opinions.
evidence.snippet:
Treatment consists of a protein-restricted diet 
combined with supplementation of carnitine and/or glycine and emergency 
treatment in catabolic episodes.

nodes.description: Secondary hyperammonemia results from inhibition of N-acetylglutamate synthase by isovaleryl-CoA. In a Jordanian cohort, hyperammonemia was present in 71.4% of patients. Repeated hyperammonemic episodes increase the risk of brain damage.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric Acidemia in Jordan.
evidence.snippet:
Laboratory results showed acidosis (81%), 
hyperammonemia (71.4%), and hypoglycemia (14.3%).

evidence.reference_title: Hyperammonaemia in classic organic acidaemias: a review of the literature and two case histories.
evidence.snippet:
Repeated and frequent episodes of hyperammonaemia 
(alongside metabolic decompensations) can result in impaired growth and 
intellectual disability, the severity of which increase with longer duration of 
hyperammonaemia.

nodes.description: Hypoglycemia may occur during metabolic crises, reported in 14.3% of patients in a Jordanian cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric Acidemia in Jordan.
evidence.snippet:
Laboratory results showed acidosis (81%), 
hyperammonemia (71.4%), and hypoglycemia (14.3%).

nodes.description: Reduced muscle tone during acute metabolic decompensation is a characteristic neurologic manifestation of classic IVA.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Isovaleric Acidemia.
evidence.snippet:
Classic IVA is characterized by acute metabolic
decompensations (vomiting, poor feeding, lethargy, hypotonia, seizures, and a
distinct odor of sweaty feet).

nodes.description: The c.941C>T (p.Ala314Val) variant in IVD is commonly associated with the attenuated or mild biochemical phenotype detected by newborn screening. Individuals homozygous for this variant may remain clinically asymptomatic.

evidence.reference_title: Machine Learning Methods Improve Specificity in Newborn Screening for Isovaleric Aciduria.
evidence.snippet:
NBS for IVA is hampered by, 
first, the increased birth prevalence due to the identification of individuals 
with an attenuated disease variant (so-called "mild" IVA)

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic variants in IVD reduce isovaleryl-CoA dehydrogenase catalytic activity in mitochondria.

evidence.reference_title: "Classical organic acidurias": diagnosis and pathogenesis.
evidence.snippet:
Among these disorders, methyl malonic aciduria, propionic aciduria, 
maple syrup urine disease and isovaleric aciduria are sometimes referred to as 
classical organic acidurias.

evidence.reference_title: IVD / isovaleric acidemia (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: IVD | HGNC:6186 | isovaleric acidemia | MONDO:0009475 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Intellectual disability may develop as a long-term consequence of recurrent metabolic crises and hyperammonemia, particularly in severe neonatal-onset forms.

delay, intellectual disability and/or impaired cognition, epilepsy, and movement

evidence.snippet:
Isovaleric acidemia (IVAcidemia) is an inborn error of metabolism due to 
deficiency of isovaleryl-CoA dehydrogenase activity, leading to predominant 
accumulation of isovaleric acid (IVA).

readouts.interpretation: Elevated isovaleric acid directly reports the toxic organic-acid pool created by impaired isovaleryl-CoA dehydrogenase flux.

evidence.reference_title: Neonatal screening for isovaleric aciduria: Reducing the increasingly high false-positive rate in Germany.
evidence.snippet:
Newborn screening (NBS) for isovaleric acidemia (IVA) is performed by flow 
injection tandem mass spectrometry quantifying C5 carnitines (C5).

readouts.interpretation: Elevated C5 isovalerylcarnitine reports carnitine conjugation of accumulated isovaleryl-CoA downstream of the IVD enzymatic block.
readouts.target: Toxic isovaleryl-CoA and organic acid accumulation

to an accumulation of isovaleric acid and its derivates 3-hydroxyisovaleric 
acid, isovaleryl (C5)-carnitine and isovalerylglycine in body fluids.

evidence.reference_title: The glycine N-acyltransferases, GLYAT and GLYATL1, contribute to the detoxification of isovaleryl-CoA - an in-silico and in vitro validation.
evidence.snippet:
The in-silico and in vitro findings suggested that both enzymes 
could form N-isovaleryglycine albeit at lower affinities than their preferred 
substrates.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated urinary isovalerylglycine reports glycine conjugation of accumulated isovaleryl-CoA and is a confirmatory IVA biomarker.
readouts.target: Toxic isovaleryl-CoA and organic acid accumulation

nodes.description: L-carnitine supplementation promotes formation and urinary excretion of isovalerylcarnitine, reducing the toxic free isovaleric acid pool and correcting secondary carnitine deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Practical Considerations for the Diagnosis and Management of Isovaleryl-CoA-Dehydrogenase Deficiency (Isovaleric Acidemia): Systematic Search and Review and Expert Opinions.
evidence.snippet:
Treatment consists of a protein-restricted diet 
combined with supplementation of carnitine and/or glycine and emergency 
treatment in catabolic episodes.

nodes.description: Reduced alertness and responsiveness during acute metabolic decompensation, ranging from somnolence to coma.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric acidemia presenting as diabetic ketoacidosis: a case report.
evidence.snippet:
Isovaleric acidemia (IVA) is characterized by periodic vomiting, lethargy, coma,
ketoacidosis and a 'sweaty feet' odor.

nodes.description: Protein-restricted or leucine-restricted dietary management with specialized metabolic formula is the cornerstone of chronic IVA management. The goal is to reduce leucine flux and thereby limit isovaleryl-CoA production.

evidence.reference_title: Practical Considerations for the Diagnosis and Management of Isovaleryl-CoA-Dehydrogenase Deficiency (Isovaleric Acidemia): Systematic Search and Review and Expert Opinions.
evidence.snippet:
Treatment consists of a protein-restricted diet 
combined with supplementation of carnitine and/or glycine and emergency 
treatment in catabolic episodes.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric Acidemia in Jordan.
evidence.snippet:
The early initiation of treatment for organic acid disorders 
carries a more favorable prognosis.

nodes.description: High-anion-gap metabolic acidosis is the hallmark acute presentation during metabolic decompensation episodes. In a Jordanian cohort, acidosis was present in 81% of patients.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric Acidemia in Jordan.
evidence.snippet:
Laboratory results showed acidosis (81%), 
hyperammonemia (71.4%), and hypoglycemia (14.3%).

evidence.reference_title: Practical Considerations for the Diagnosis and Management of Isovaleryl-CoA-Dehydrogenase Deficiency (Isovaleric Acidemia): Systematic Search and Review and Expert Opinions.
evidence.snippet:
The 
clinical presentation is highly variable, ranging from life-threatening 
metabolic crises with metabolic acidosis and hyperammonemia to a clinically 
asymptomatic only biochemical phenotype.

nodes.description: Accumulated isovaleryl-CoA and isovaleric acid impair tricarboxylic acid cycle function, with evidence for inhibition of citrate synthase and reduced CO2 production from acetate in brain tissue. Chronic metabolite accumulation also increases oxidative stress, contributing to neurological morbidity. Oxidative stress from chronic persistent buildup of isovaleric acid may be more neurologically detrimental than acute buildup.
nodes.id: Mitochondrial energy depletion and oxidative stress

evidence.reference_title: Creatine administration prevents Na+,K+-ATPase inhibition induced by intracerebroventricular administration of isovaleric acid in cerebral cortex of young rats.
evidence.snippet:
IVA administration significantly inhibited (14)CO(2) production from 
acetate (22%) and citrate synthase activity (20%) in cerebral cortex homogenates 
prepared 24 h after injection.

evidence.reference_title: The glycine N-acyltransferases, GLYAT and GLYATL1, contribute to the detoxification of isovaleryl-CoA - an in-silico and in vitro validation.
evidence.snippet:
The increase in these metabolites decreases mitochondrial energy 
production and increases oxidative stress.

nodes.description: Movement disorders such as tremor, dysmetria, and extrapyramidal movements are reported as additional manifestations of classic IVA, likely reflecting metabolic brain injury from severe or recurrent decompensations.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Isovaleric Acidemia.
evidence.snippet:
Additional manifestations of classic IVA include developmental
delay, intellectual disability and/or impaired cognition, epilepsy, and movement
disorder (tremor, dysmetria, extrapyramidal movements).

nodes.description: N-carbamylglutamate is a synthetic analog of N-acetylglutamate approved for treatment of hyperammonemia in IVA. It directly activates CPS1, bypassing the NAGS inhibition caused by isovaleryl-CoA, and can rapidly normalize ammonia levels.

evidence.reference_title: Hyperammonaemia in classic organic acidaemias: a review of the literature and two case histories.
evidence.snippet:
Treatment with N-carbamyl-L-glutamate can rapidly normalise 
ammonia levels by stimulating the first step of the urea cycle.

nodes.description: Isovaleric acid directly inhibits Na+,K+-ATPase activity in cerebral cortex, a crucial enzyme for maintaining membrane potential and normal neurotransmission. This inhibition is observed as early as 2 hours after exposure and persists at 24 hours, contributing to the acute encephalopathy seen during metabolic crises.
nodes.id: Neuronal membrane dysfunction via Na+,K+-ATPase inhibition

evidence.reference_title: Creatine administration prevents Na+,K+-ATPase inhibition induced by intracerebroventricular administration of isovaleric acid in cerebral cortex of young rats.
evidence.snippet:
IVA injection significantly inhibited 
Na(+),K(+)-ATPase activity (25%) in cerebral cortex of rats 2 or 24 h after IVA 
administration, while pre-treatment of rats with creatine completely prevented 
the inhibitory effects of IVA on Na(+),K(+)-ATPase.

nodes.description: Pancytopenia and neutropenia may occur during acute metabolic decompensation in organic acidemias including IVA, due to bone marrow suppression by toxic metabolites.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric acidemia: report of one case.
evidence.snippet:
A case of isovaleric acidemia appearing as diabetic ketoacidosis with acute
encephalopathy and pancytopenia was reported.

nodes.description: Isovaleryl-CoA inhibits N-acetylglutamate synthase (NAGS), reducing N-acetylglutamate availability and impairing activation of carbamoyl phosphate synthetase 1 (CPS1), the rate-limiting entry enzyme of the urea cycle. Combined with acetyl-CoA depletion, this produces secondary hyperammonemia that contributes to encephalopathy and neurological complications.
nodes.id: Secondary urea cycle impairment and hyperammonemia

evidence.reference_title: Organic acid disorders.
evidence.snippet:
High anion gap metabolic acidosis with 
hyperammonemia is a characteristic OA biochemical finding.

evidence.reference_title: Practical Considerations for the Diagnosis and Management of Isovaleryl-CoA-Dehydrogenase Deficiency (Isovaleric Acidemia): Systematic Search and Review and Expert Opinions.
evidence.snippet:
The 
clinical presentation is highly variable, ranging from life-threatening 
metabolic crises with metabolic acidosis and hyperammonemia to a clinically 
asymptomatic only biochemical phenotype.

nodes.description: Seizures may occur during severe metabolic decompensation, likely mediated by hyperammonemia and direct neurotoxicity of accumulated metabolites.

evidence.reference_title: Classic Isovaleric Acidemia.
evidence.snippet:
Classic IVA is characterized by acute metabolic
decompensations (vomiting, poor feeding, lethargy, hypotonia, seizures, and a
distinct odor of sweaty feet).

nodes.description: Blocked isovaleryl-CoA oxidation causes accumulation of isovaleryl-CoA, isovaleric acid, 3-hydroxyisovaleric acid, isovalerylcarnitine, and isovalerylglycine. This toxic organic acid burden drives the characteristic odor, secondary detoxification through carnitine and glycine conjugation, and downstream acute metabolic decompensation, hyperammonemia, mitochondrial stress, and direct neurotoxicity.
nodes.id: Toxic isovaleryl-CoA and organic acid accumulation

evidence.reference_title: Practical Considerations for the Diagnosis and Management of Isovaleryl-CoA-Dehydrogenase Deficiency (Isovaleric Acidemia): Systematic Search and Review and Expert Opinions.
evidence.snippet:
Isovaleric acidemia (IVA, OMIM 243500) is an inherited disorder of leucine 
metabolism caused by a deficiency of isovaleryl-CoA dehydrogenase (IVD), leading 
to an accumulation of isovaleric acid and its derivates 3-hydroxyisovaleric 
acid, isovaleryl (C5)-carnitine and isovalerylglycine in body fluids.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric acidemia: Therapeutic response to supplementation with glycine, l-carnitine, or both in combination and a 10-year follow-up case study.
evidence.snippet:
Deficiency of this enzyme leads to accumulation of
organic acids, such as isovalerylcarnitine and isovalerylglycine.

nodes.description: Multiple pathogenic variants in IVD have been described causing variable clinical severity from severe neonatal-onset to chronic intermittent forms. Biallelic pathogenic variants are required for disease manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Practical Considerations for the Diagnosis and Management of Isovaleryl-CoA-Dehydrogenase Deficiency (Isovaleric Acidemia): Systematic Search and Review and Expert Opinions.
evidence.snippet:
Isovaleric acidemia (IVA, OMIM 243500) is an inherited disorder of leucine 
metabolism caused by a deficiency of isovaleryl-CoA dehydrogenase (IVD)

nodes.description: Recurrent vomiting is the most prevalent symptom during metabolic crises in IVA. In a Jordanian cohort, vomiting was the most common presenting symptom at 57.1%.

evidence.reference_title: Isovaleric Acidemia in Jordan.
evidence.snippet:
Vomiting was the most prevalent symptom (57.1%), and 
encephalopathy occurred in 33.3%.
